verdict.answer_first: B states the actionable meaning immediately: "ACID is a set of four guarantees... so your data stays correct even when things go wrong"; A also answers early but is more structured/background-heavy.
verdict.decisiveness: B commits more directly with "Reach for it whenever incorrect data would actually hurt"; A surveys with "It's a design trade-off" and offers follow-up topics.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on now: it gives the practical rule in one line, "Reach for it whenever incorrect data would actually hurt," with examples.
